result:
If I could change the world in one way, I’d make it so that every person could truly feel the inner life of others — not abstractly, not as a moral lesson, but directly enough that cruelty became difficult to sustain.

Not perfect empathy. Not universal agreement. Just enough shared perception that a hungry child, a terrified refugee, a lonely elder, an exploited worker, or even an enemy stopped being an idea and became unmistakably real.

I think a lot of the world’s worst problems survive because distance protects people from the consequences of what they tolerate, buy, vote for, ignore, or inflict. If that distance collapsed — emotionally, not geographically — many other changes would follow: less war, less dehumanization, less indifference, less casual destruction.

So my one change would be: make human beings unable to forget that other people are fully real.
